what_it_does: Renders a collapsible legend/orientation panel for the surface instrument, choosing a human-readable title and explanation lines from a projection binding's declared meta in the default unmodified state and recomputing them from the live projection, view mode, centre, and scrubbed time when the operator changes the reference frame.
observations: Collapsible legend with localStorage persistence key instrument.legend.collapsed; title stays visible when collapsed.
Uses binding.meta fields (name/is/fills/why) for the default, un-centred, live view.
Falls back to computed describe() when re-centred or time-scrubbed so the legend does not misstate radius or time meaning.
describe() covers square grid, nucleation, separator, semantic (coarse rung and centred strain field), edge-bearing lenses, and a default time/address reading.
asOf() converts an ISO timestamp into a locale-formatted 'as of' string.
angleMeaning() maps angle_from values to plain-English sector meanings.
For centred semantic views it counts outward vs inward points to label warm/cool strain populations.
In 'both' view it appends a lens-neutral note about diamonds on the axes where grid and circle meet.
Uses stamp('ui://instrument/legend') to attach a data attribute.
Toggle button carries aria-expanded and aria-label for accessibility.
standouts: The explicit guard that bypasses static meta when s.centre or s.at is set, keeping the legend truthful after re-centre or time scrub.
The centred semantic strain-field decoding with warm/cool population counts.
LocalStorage-backed collapse preference with graceful fallback when storage is blocked.
conventions: React functional component with useState and a side-effect-on-event toggle pattern.
TypeScript imports types from sibling modules rather than declaring them locally.
localStorage reads/writes are wrapped in try/catch for privacy/blocking modes.
Uses a stamp() helper to attach a semantic data attribute.
Comments are verbose, timestamped, and framed as operator-law requirements.
Avoids hardcoded per-lens prose by deriving text from binding fields.
concerns: The warm/cool point-counting loop runs on every render and could be memoized.
No storage event listener, so collapse state will not sync across browser tabs.
The meta.is truthy check could silently skip meta if the field is missing or empty.
angleMeaning only knows a few angle_from values; others fall through to a generic replace that may read awkwardly.
The edge-bearing branch is ordered after radius_from branches, so some edge cases may be shadowed.
The 'both' view diamond line is appended outside describe(), so it is not available in the meta path.
notes: This is the operator-facing legend for the surface instrument, with a safety switch that recomputes its text when the operator re-centres or scrubs to a past moment.
questions: What is the exact shape and population timing of Projection.binding.meta?
What are all possible radius_from values and how is each determined?
What does stamp('ui://instrument/legend') produce and where is it consumed?
What are the full ViewMode values and how do they affect rendering elsewhere?
How are strain, r_struct, scale_size, and r computed in the projection?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the file is a collapsible legend that explains the active lens, and it adds explicit handling to keep descriptions accurate when the view is re-centred or scrubbed to the past.
apparent_themes: Operator-law UX: never cryptic, always say what the view is and what it chose.
Registry-true meaning: human text derived from binding metadata, not hardcoded per lens.
Runtime truthfulness: static descriptions are bypassed when runtime state changes their meaning.
intention_signals: Make the visualization self-explanatory by binding human-readable meaning to the current projection state, while protecting against stale descriptions when the operator changes the centre or time reference.
novelty: The explicit 'meta when default, computed describe when modified' guard against stale radius/time semantics, plus the warm/cool strain population count for the centred semantic lens.
contribution: A centralized, runtime-aware legend generator that translates projection bindings and runtime state into human-readable orientation text, with persistence for the operator's collapse preference.
summary_for_embedding: Legend.tsx is a React component that renders a collapsible, localStorage-persisted orientation panel for a surface visualization, selecting human-readable title and explanation lines from a projection binding's declared meta in the default state and recomputing them from the live projection, view mode, centre, and scrubbed time when the operator changes the reference frame.
intention_for_embedding: It was intended to keep operators continuously oriented about what the current lens shows and what its visual encodings mean, even after re-centring or scrubbing to a past moment.

symbols.does: Maps an angle_from binding field to a human phrase describing what the angular sectors represent.
symbols.description: Returns a short, plain-English description of the angle encoding based on the binding's angle_from value, handling special cases like 'kind', 'kind-group', and empty values, and falling back to a generic sector-per-value reading.

symbols.does: Formats an ISO timestamp into a readable 'as of' moment for the operator.
symbols.description: Parses the provided timestamp and returns a locale-formatted date/time string; if parsing fails, it returns the fallback phrase 'an earlier moment'.

symbols.does: Computes title and explanatory lines for the active projection based on lens type, view mode, centre, and scrubbed time.
symbols.description: Branches on view mode and radius_from to produce registry-true orientation text for square grid, nucleation, separator, semantic (including coarse rung and centred strain-field decoding), edge-bearing, and default time/address lenses; prepends past-time context when s.at is set.

symbols.does: Renders a collapsible legend panel that explains the current surface view.
symbols.description: Maintains a localStorage-persisted collapsed state, chooses between the binding's declared meta description (when un-centred and live) and the computed describe() output (when re-centred, time-scrubbed, or in 'both' view), and renders the title with a toggle and a list of explanation lines.

symbols.does: Toggles the legend's collapsed state and persists the preference.
symbols.description: Flips the collapsed boolean, writes '1' or '0' to localStorage under COLLAPSE_KEY with a try/catch for blocked storage, and updates component state.
